posts.title: Building an AI-Powered Vocabulary Learning App
posts.description: Design notes from an adaptive vocabulary learning system that uses feedback loops and context-aware practice.
posts.body:
## The Problem Space

Traditional vocabulary learning often relies on static flashcards, fixed review schedules, and decontextualized word lists. I am interested in a system that notices how each learner remembers and forgets individual words.

## Design Goals

- Adaptive review schedules
- Contextual examples instead of isolated words
- Active production practice
- Feedback that explains why an answer works or fails

The larger question is how language technology can support learning without flattening the complexity of real language use.

posts.title: Computational Approaches to Speech Analysis
posts.description: A compact map of signal processing, pitch tracking, and computational methods for speech research.
posts.body:
## The Speech Signal

Speech is a time-varying acoustic signal carrying linguistic and paralinguistic information. Computational analysis lets us inspect timing, spectral structure, pitch, rhythm, and intensity at scale.

## Useful Layers

- Acoustic level: waveform, energy, spectral properties
- Phonetic level: segments, timing, voicing
- Prosodic level: pitch contours, rhythm, stress patterns

For my work, the most interesting layer is often the bridge between acoustic measurement and linguistic interpretation.

posts.title: Why Prosody Research Matters
posts.description: A reflection on pitch, rhythm, stress, and the parts of speech that written words do not fully capture.
posts.body:
## Beyond Words

Prosody carries information that written text cannot fully show: emphasis, grouping, stance, timing, and affect. These patterns shape how listeners interpret speech.

## Research Direction

I am especially interested in how prosodic cues differ across speakers and languages, and how computational tools can make those differences visible without reducing them to a single number.
